headline: Next Steps & Action Items
subheadline: What needs to happen before committing capital — prioritized and assigned

phaseOverview.description: We have two viable development scenarios and a strong understanding of the by-law landscape. The next phase is confirming the open regulatory questions, firming up the pro forma, and making the Scenario A vs. B decision.

phases.name: Phase 1: Regulatory Confirmation
phases.description: Resolve open by-law questions with Building Inspector and Planning Dept

phases.name: Phase 2: Design & Feasibility
phases.description: Engage architect to test buildable envelope, refine unit mix, develop concept design

phases.name: Phase 3: Financial Commitment
phases.description: Firm pro forma, secure financing LOI, present to investors

phases.name: Phase 4: Acquisition & Entitlement
phases.description: Close acquisition, file permits, begin community engagement

items.action: Schedule meeting with Ted Murray (Building Inspector)
items.purpose: Walk through Sections 4.08, 5.21, 6, and Table 5.01 in person. Confirm side setback formula, IZ rounding rules, Transit Overlay boundary, and Section 5.21 public benefit applicability.
items.notes: Emily Noel recommended this meeting and will forward your email to Ted. Plan for in-person meeting after reviewing the referenced by-law sections.

items.action: Order lot survey (width x depth dimensions)
items.purpose: Cannot model the buildable envelope without knowing lot width. The side setback formula (Length/10 + 10) makes this critical for determining whether 14 units physically fit.
items.notes: A basic boundary survey is sufficient for this stage. Full ALTA survey can wait until closer to acquisition.

items.purpose: Determine: (1) rounding rules for fractional units (2.1 units = 2 or 3?), (2) current payment-in-lieu fee amount, (3) whether affordable unit bedrooms must match proportional bedroom count, (4) current 80% AMI sale price for Brookline.
items.notes: The payment-in-lieu fee may be more favorable than on-site affordable units, especially for Beatka's plan. The $25,000 'rebate for A9' in Amr's spreadsheet may relate to this.

items.purpose: Verify 19 Linden St is within the Transit Overlay Parking District (0.5 miles from Brookline Village station). If yes, parking drops from 1.4-2.0 to 1.0 space per unit.
items.notes: Almost certainly qualifies given proximity, but need official confirmation.

items.action: Contact Preservation Department about demo delay applicability
items.purpose: Confirm: (1) does adaptive reuse (keeping the house) avoid the 18-month delay entirely?, (2) does splitting the house into 2 units (Amr's plan) trigger the 'systematic removal of character-defining elements' clause?, (3) what level of interior renovation is allowed without triggering the delay?
items.notes: Emily Noel specifically suggested reaching out to the preservation department on the demo delay question.

items.purpose: Test buildable envelope with actual lot dimensions and setback formula. Develop concept plans for both Scenario A (14 units) and Scenario B (5-6 units). Confirm whether 14 units + underground garage fit within the envelope.
items.notes: Architect should have experience with National Register properties and Brookline permitting. Emily Noel suggested reviewing Section 5.21 'prior to design.'

items.action: Update Amr's financial model with by-law adjustments
items.purpose: Incorporate: (1) Section 4.08 affordable units or payment-in-lieu, (2) parking cost as explicit line item, (3) fix 162 sqft FAR overage, (4) clarify the 1,200 sqft 'second existing unit', (5) model both scenarios side-by-side.
items.notes: Current model shows $7.5M profit but doesn't include IZ adjustment (~$2.2M reduction) or explicit parking costs.

items.action: Research Section 5.21 public benefit incentive in detail
items.purpose: Determine if preserving the Greek Revival house qualifies for FAR/height exceptions. If yes, this could unlock additional sellable sqft — transformative for Scenario A which is at max FAR.
items.notes: Discuss at Ted Murray meeting first, then follow up with zoning attorney if promising.

items.action: Get broker pricing opinion (BPO) for both unit mixes
items.purpose: Beatka can provide a formal BPO for the 5-6 unit luxury plan. Need a second opinion on Amr's $1,500/sqft assumption for 1,000 sqft units. Compare against recent 58 Kent ($1,396/sqft) and Solaire ($1,000-1,333/sqft).
items.notes: Beatka has the strongest local comp knowledge — she sold 40 Kent and 40 Webster Place nearby.

items.action: Check MBTA Communities Act Section 3A overlay map
items.purpose: Determine if 19 Linden is within the overlay boundary. If yes, future density upside beyond M-1.0 limits — particularly valuable for Beatka's plan which only uses 5 of 15 current units.
items.notes: Brookline adopted compliance zoning Fall 2023. Maps should be available from Planning Dept.

items.action: Finalize pro forma for chosen scenario
items.purpose: Lock in all cost and revenue assumptions with by-law adjustments, architect's buildable envelope confirmation, and broker pricing opinion. This becomes the investor presentation pro forma.

items.action: Make Scenario A vs. B decision
items.purpose: Based on: (1) buildable envelope confirmation, (2) IZ rounding/payment-in-lieu terms, (3) BPO for each unit mix, (4) community risk appetite. May also consider a hybrid (e.g., 8-10 units).
Review entitlement pathway for chosen scenario. Confirm Section 5.21 applicability. Prepare for pre-application conference with Planning Board.
1843 structure likely contains lead paint and possibly asbestos. Required for acquisition diligence and financing.

items.action: Title search and deed restriction review
items.purpose: Confirm no easements, restrictions, or encumbrances that affect development potential.
Compile finalized pro forma, by-law analysis, architect concept, and risk matrix into investor-ready materials. Update Lovable site.

contacts.name: Ted Murray
contacts.context: Emily Noel will forward your email. He can walk through Sections 4.08, 5.21, 6, Table 5.01. Plan in-person meeting.

contacts.name: Emily Noel
contacts.context: Initial contact who provided regulatory guidance and the referral to Ted Murray.

contacts.name: Beatka Zakrzewski
contacts.context: Sold 40 Kent and 40 Webster Place (1 block away, both NR-listed). Deep local comp knowledge. Proposed the 5-6 unit luxury strategy.

contacts.name: Amr
contacts.context: Built the pro forma spreadsheet. Proposed the 14-unit density strategy. Model needs updating for Section 4.08 IZ adjustment.

contacts.name: Brookline Preservation Department
contacts.context: Confirm whether adaptive reuse avoids the 18-month delay. Discuss the house-split question for Scenario A.

contacts.name: Brookline Planning Department
contacts.context: Pre-application conference recommended. Can confirm IZ rounding rules, payment-in-lieu fees, and MBTA overlay map.

questions.question: How does Section 4.08 round fractional affordable units? Is 2.1 rounded to 2 or 3?
What is the current payment-in-lieu fee for Section 4.08?
questions.impact: May be more favorable than on-site affordable units, especially for Scenario B

questions.question: What are the lot dimensions (width x depth)?
questions.impact: Determines buildable envelope after side setbacks. Blocks architect engagement.

questions.question: Does the existing house split (3,000 + 1,200 sqft) trigger the demolition delay?
questions.impact: If yes, Scenario A loses the 'no demo delay' advantage (adds ~$660K carry + 18 months)

questions.question: Is underground parking included in Amr's $7M building cost?
questions.impact: If not, add $1.05-1.4M to Scenario A costs (reducing profit to ~$3.9-4.2M with IZ)

questions.question: Does Section 5.21 public benefit incentive apply to historic preservation?
questions.impact: If yes, could unlock FAR > 1.0 or height > 40ft — additional sellable sqft

questions.question: Is 19 Linden within the Transit Overlay Parking District?
questions.impact: If yes, parking drops to 1.0 space/unit, saving $350-700K in garage costs

questions.question: Is 19 Linden within the MBTA Communities Act Section 3A overlay?
questions.impact: If yes, future density upside beyond M-1.0 — valuable optionality

questions.question: What does the $25,000 'rebate for A9' in Amr's spreadsheet refer to?
questions.impact: May relate to Section 4.08 or another incentive/fee. Needs clarification.
